### Post by Jeztastic on 2008-05-11
I have tried using the psychocats guide, and searched the forum. I am running Ubuntu using a wubi install. I have two windows partitions - Vista and Data.

fstab;

```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
/host/ubuntu/disks/root.disk / ext3 loop,errors=remount-ro 0 1
/host/ubuntu/disks/boot /boot none bind 0 0
/host/ubuntu/disks/swap.disk none swap loop,sw 0 0
/dev/hda /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
/dev/sda1 /home/DVD ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_GB.UTF-8 0 0
/dev/sda2 /home/Vista ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_GB.UTF-8 0 0
/dev/sda3 /home/Data ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_GB.UTF-8 0 0
```

I cannot get the drives to mount properly. They appeared in /home, but I could not write to them, and Ubuntu was telling me they were not mounted. They did not appear on the desktop. 

Please help!
